jobs.title: Program Manager
jobs.description:
**PROGRAM MANAGER – DDA SERVICES** 

**Location:** Maryland (Remote)

**Job Type:** Full\-Time

**Reports To:** Executive Director

**POSITION SUMMARY**

We are seeking an experienced and well\-connected **Program Manager – DDA Services** to lead the growth of a licensed Maryland Developmental Disabilities Administration (DDA) provider agency.

This leadership position is ideal for a former Coordinator of Community Services (CCS), Targeted Case Manager, or experienced DDA professional who has extensive knowledge of Maryland's DDA system, Person\-Centered Planning, LTSS Maryland, and DDA waiver programs. The ideal candidate has established professional relationships throughout Maryland's developmental disabilities community and is passionate about connecting individuals and families with high\-quality, person\-centered services.

The Program Manager will serve as the agency's primary liaison to Coordinators of Community Services (CCS), families, transition coordinators, and community partners to increase awareness of our services and support participant enrollment. In addition, this position provides operational leadership by overseeing DDA waiver services, ensuring regulatory compliance, supervising staff, and promoting exceptional participant outcomes.

**RESPONSIBILITIES**

**Community Partnerships, Participant Recruitment \& Agency Growth**

· Serve as the agency's primary liaison to Coordinators of Community Services (CCS), support brokers, transition coordinators, behavioral health providers, and other community partners.

· Develop and maintain strong professional relationships that increase awareness of the agency's DDA waiver services.

· Conduct outreach meetings, presentations, networking events, and community engagement activities to promote agency services.

· Meet with prospective participants and families to explain available DDA services and coordinate participant intake and admissions.

· Maintain ongoing communication with referral partners regarding agency services, participant needs, and program availability.

· Represent the agency at DDA provider meetings, resource fairs, and professional networking events.

**Program Operations \& Leadership**

· Collaborate with CCS professionals, families, nurses, behavioral professionals, and interdisciplinary teams to ensure coordinated, person\-centered services.

· Oversee the delivery of DDA waiver services in accordance with each individual's Person\-Centered Plan (PCP).

· Supervise program staff, provide coaching and performance management, and promote a culture of accountability and excellence.

· Monitor participant satisfaction, service quality, health and safety, and program outcomes.

· Ensure compliance with Maryland DDA regulations, COMAR requirements, Medicaid waiver standards, and agency policies.

**QUALIFICATIONS**

**Preferred**

· Former Coordinator of Community Services (CCS) or Targeted Case Manager.

· Experience managing DDA waiver programs within a provider agency.

· Established professional relationships within Maryland's developmental disabilities community.

· Experience with participant intake, admissions, and service coordination.

· Familiarity with HRST, MAST, Electronic Visit Verification (EVV), Incident Management, and DDA compliance requirements.

**Required**

· Bachelor's degree in Human Services, Social Work, Psychology, Healthcare Administration, or a related field.

· Minimum five (5\) years of experience working within Maryland's Developmental Disabilities Administration (DDA) system.

· Strong knowledge of Maryland DDA waiver programs, Person\-Centered Planning, LTSS Maryland, and community resources.

· Excellent relationship\-building, networking, communication, and leadership skills.

· Valid Maryland driver's license and reliable transportation.

**What Success Looks Like**

The successful Program Manager will establish strong partnerships with CCS agencies and community organizations, increase awareness of the agency throughout Maryland, support participant enrollment and retention, maintain high\-quality DDA waiver services, and ensure full compliance with DDA, Medicaid, and COMAR requirements while helping build an organization recognized for excellence, integrity, and person\-centered care.

**Equal Opportunity Employer**

We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities and fostering an inclusive workplace where every individual is treated with dignity, respect, and fairness.

Pay: $30\.00 \- $45\.00 per hour

Work Location: Remote

jobs.company: Covered By Love Inc. dba CBL Enterprises
jobs.title: Program Manager – U.S. Army EFMP Respite Care Program
jobs.description:
About CBL Enterprises

CBL Enterprises is a federal government contractor dedicated to delivering high\-quality staffing, program management, and operational support services to government agencies nationwide. We are seeking experienced professionals who are committed to serving military families through the U.S. Army Exceptional Family Member Program (EFMP).

Position Summary

The Program Manager is responsible for the overall management, administration, and successful execution of the EFMP Respite Care Services contract. This individual will oversee daily operations, ensure compliance with contract requirements, supervise key personnel, maintain quality assurance, and serve as the primary liaison with the Government.

Responsibilities

* Manage daily contract operations.
* Supervise Regional Coordinators.
* Ensure compliance with the Performance Work Statement (PWS).
* Maintain quality assurance and performance standards.
* Oversee staffing and operational readiness.
* Prepare required reports.
* Participate in Government meetings.
* Monitor contract performance.
* Support continuous process improvement.
* Ensure exceptional customer service for military families.

Minimum Qualifications

* Bachelor's degree from an accredited college or university.
* Minimum of five (5\) years of program or project management experience.
* Experience leading multidisciplinary teams.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
* Strong organizational and leadership abilities.
* Proficient with Microsoft Office Suite.

Preferred Qualifications

* Federal Government contracting experience.
* Department of Defense experience.
* Healthcare, Human Services, or Military Family Program experience.
* Experience managing geographically dispersed teams.

**Employment is contingent upon contract award and successful completion of all required pre\-employment requirements.**

Alternate Program Manager

Use the **same location information**, changing only the title and pay.

Pay

* **Fort Liberty:** **$36\.48/hour**
* **Fort Belvoir:** **$45\.52/hour**
* **Joint Base San Antonio:** **$38\.42/hour**
* **MacDill AFB:** **$35\.95/hour**

The duties are the same as previously drafted, emphasizing support to the Program Manager and acting in their absence.

Pay: $35\.95 \- $45\.52 per hour

Work Location: Remote
